mode: --changed-files (scope: cycle diff only; Mechanism (ii) inspector-side tagging via introduced_in_diff)

not_in_changed_files_list: INDEX.md (auto-generated by doc-sync; reflects in-scope hook + doc additions)
hooks/INDEX.md (auto-generated by doc-sync; reflects in-scope hook additions)
docs/reference/INDEX.md (auto-generated by doc-sync; reflects in-scope doc addition)

verification_summary.hooks_location_correct: hooks/ matches existing siblings session-info.sh, session-git-init.sh, session-promote-hook.sh, userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh, userprompt-doc-sync-check.py — same naming convention (event-prefix-purpose.sh)
verification_summary.doc_location_correct: docs/reference/ matches existing siblings server-infra.md, checkpoint-mechanism.md, lock-file-handling.md, configuration-summary.md, fswatch-quickref.md
verification_summary.no_duplicates: no .bak / .tmp / .old / ~ siblings found alongside new files
verification_summary.executable_bits: both .sh hooks have execute bit set (-rwxr-xr-x)

summary.verdict: clean — all in-scope changed files are correctly placed, named per existing conventions, properly cross-referenced, and free of duplicates / temp files / build artifacts

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 1 ISSUE (scope coverage of auto-generated INDEX files) and 2 OBSERVATION_ONLYs confirming the empty-findings verdict. No Safety-Rules violation flagged. No false negative within the actual --changed-files scope.

findings.issue: INDEX.md, hooks/INDEX.md, and docs/reference/INDEX.md were modified during this cycle (visible in git status) but are not in the explicit --changed-files list, so the audit may have under-covered the full cycle diff if those index updates were introduced this cycle.
findings.proposed_fix: Add INDEX.md, hooks/INDEX.md, and docs/reference/INDEX.md to parameters.changed_files, or explicitly mark them pre-existing/out-of-scope.
findings.rationale: Per the --changed-files scope contract (Section 5.4 / AC-12.1), this inspector only emits findings for files explicitly listed in --changed-files. The three INDEX.md files are auto-generated by doc-sync hooks reacting to the in-scope additions (new hook files + new doc file); they are not authored content. Spot-check of the diffs (via git diff) confirms they consist solely of mechanical doc-sync regeneration adding entries for the three new in-scope files plus pre-existing unrelated entries (test_allowlist_consolidation.py was already untracked before this cycle). No cleanliness issue is hidden by their exclusion from the explicit scope. Logging the scope clarification per orchestrator default-safe contract; no audit re-run required.

findings.issue: Draft has no Safety-Rules violation — no deletes or relocations proposed, so the <7d / functional-reference safety rules are not triggered.
findings.rationale: Codex confirms the draft's empty findings do not implicate any Safety Rule. Recorded as positive confirmation.

findings.issue: /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh superficially matches the tmp-*.sh one-time-script pattern but is a functional system cleanup script at an appropriate system path; leaving all categories empty is not a false negative.
findings.rationale: Codex confirms the deliberate out-of-repo placement of /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh is correct and matches the orchestrator's stated intent. Not a finding.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex's single ISSUE was a scope-coverage observation, not a content finding. Incorporated by adding a scope.not_in_changed_files_list section to the report documenting that the three auto-generated INDEX files are causally tied to the in-scope additions but fall outside the explicit --changed-files contract per AC-12.1 default-safe rule. Spot-checked their diffs via `git diff` to confirm no hidden cleanliness issues; diffs are pure doc-sync regeneration. Both OBSERVATION_ONLYs are logged as positive confirmation. Empty findings verdict retained.
